body:
We still have a performance issue related to the implicit solver. It is very difficult to diagnose. I tried to remove splatting where possible and the `Val(tracer_index)` to see if that creates weird problems. This PR is open just to see if the tests speed up with this change. 

Edit: this PR also allows to use the `discrete_form` diffusivity with `field` dependency, which were not previously passed to the implicit solver

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Clean up unnecessary splatting and allow `fields` to be passed in the implicit solver

body:
Previously, `VarianceDissipation` included the computation of gradients squared to be able to compute effective diffusivities.

This was changed when refactoring this method in #4522

However, the `gradients_squared` fields remained in the `VarianceDissipation` type. This PR cleans up the tyoe removing thee vestiagial fields.
title: Clean up `VarianceDissipation`

body:
I am afraid that, given the very low values given to velocity, and an almost constant temperature and salinity, the benchmark tests do not really run in a fair way. I had already noticed a case where the performance depended on the initial conditions (i.e. if your velocities are zeros, the kernel automatically does not compute the reconstructions).

This PR changes the initial conditions of the benchmark tests to make sure they are fair, i.e. a stratified fluid in z with much higher velocity fluctuations that cannot die out in the first iteration

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Improve benchmark tests
